expectations.text: References at least one script by filename
expectations.evidence: References 6 scripts by filename: slo_calculator.py (Section 3), alert_quality_checker.py (Section 4), dashboard_generator.py (Section 7), health_check_validator.py (Section 8), analyze_metrics.py and log_analyzer.py (Section 11 table).

Multiple runnable CLI commands provided, e.g.: 'python3 monitoring-observability/scripts/slo_calculator.py --table', 'python3 monitoring-observability/scripts/dashboard_generator.py webapp --title "Payment Service" --service payment-service --output dashboards/payment-service.json', 'python3 monitoring-observability/scripts/alert_quality_checker.py /path/to/alert-rules/', 'python3 monitoring-observability/scripts/health_check_validator.py https://service.internal/healthz https://service.internal/ready --verbose'.

expectations.text: Links to a relevant reference file
expectations.evidence: Section 12 'Key Reference Documents' explicitly lists 6 reference files: metrics_design.md, alerting_best_practices.md, logging_guide.md, tracing_guide.md, slo_sla_guide.md, and tool_comparison.md, all under the references/ directory.

Response is organized into 12 numbered sections following a systematic approach: Tool Selection (1) -> Metrics Strategy (2) -> SLOs (3) -> Alerting (4) -> Log Aggregation (5) -> Distributed Tracing (6) -> Dashboards (7) -> Health Checks (8) -> On-Call/Incident Response (9) -> Implementation Roadmap (10) -> Scripts (11) -> References (12). This mirrors the monitoring-observability skill's decision-tree workflow pattern (Setup from scratch -> tool selection -> metrics -> alerting -> SLOs).

Section 10 provides a detailed 4-week implementation roadmap with phased steps: Week 1 (Foundation: deploy kube-prometheus-stack, Loki, Tempo, OTel Collector, PagerDuty), Week 2 (Application Instrumentation: structured logging, Prometheus metrics, OTel auto-instrumentation, health checks), Week 3 (Dashboards, Alerts, SLOs), Week 4 (Validation and Hardening). Each week contains 4-8 numbered checkbox items.
